skill.name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
skill.description: Configures repository-level guidance for Matt Pocock engineering skills, including issue tracker choice, triage labels, and domain documentation paths.
skill.summary: Set up issue tracker, triage label, and domain documentation context for Matt Pocock engineering skills.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak crypto patterns, but the reviewed files are Markdown skill instructions and seed templates. The command-like text is user-facing guidance for gh, glab, git, and markdown files, with no executable script, hidden network call, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt found.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Issue Tracker CLI Operations
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use gh and glab for issue creation, comments, labels, and closure. This is expected behavior, but it can modify external issue trackers when authenticated.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly describe gh and glab operations. They are not hidden execution paths, but authenticated CLI use can mutate remote trackers.

low_findings.title: Repository File Writes During Setup
low_findings.description: The skill writes or updates AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md and creates docs under docs/agents. These writes are disclosed and require user confirmation during the setup flow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The setup workflow clearly names the files it may edit or create. The instructions include a confirmation step before writing.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick and weak crypto alerts come from Markdown inline code, fenced examples, file extensions, and CLI option text. No executable Ruby, shell script, or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these locations are Markdown documentation or examples. They do not contain executable code paths or crypto calls.

low_findings.title: Documentation Link To External CLI Site
low_findings.description: The hardcoded GitLab URL points to official glab CLI documentation in Markdown link text. It is not fetched by the skill and does not transmit repository data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only as a documentation link for the GitLab CLI. There is no code that sends requests to the URL.

content.value_statement: Engineering skills often fail when they do not know where issues, labels, and domain docs live. This skill records those repository conventions so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can use the right workflow.
actual_capabilities: Detects whether a repository already has AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md guidance.
Helps choose GitHub, GitLab, local markdown, or another issue tracker workflow.
Creates docs/agents files for issue tracker, triage labels, and domain docs.
Maps five canonical triage roles to repository-specific label names.
Documents single-context or multi-context domain documentation layouts.
Updates an existing Agent skills section instead of adding a duplicate.
limitations: It is a prompt-driven setup guide, not an automated installer script.
It depends on user confirmation before writing repository guidance files.
It does not create GitHub or GitLab labels by itself.
It records custom trackers as prose when no built-in template exists.

use_cases.title: Prepare a repository for engineering agents
use_cases.description: Add the shared issue tracker, triage, and domain documentation context before using implementation skills.

use_cases.title: Standardize triage labels across tools
use_cases.description: Map existing tracker labels to the canonical roles used by Matt Pocock engineering skills.

use_cases.title: Support monorepo domain context
use_cases.description: Record whether the project uses one context file or a context map with per-area documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Run the basic setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Matt Pocock skills for this repository. Inspect the existing guidance files and ask me each setup question one at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Configure GitHub issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Use GitHub as the issue tracker.
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure this repository to use GitHub Issues for Matt Pocock engineering skills. Keep the default triage labels unless you find existing alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Use local markdown issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Use local files instead of a hosted tracker.
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Matt Pocock skills with local markdown issues under .scratch. Show me the docs/agents draft before writing files.

prompt_templates.title: Document a multi-context monorepo
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced setup for multiple domain contexts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Matt Pocock skills for a multi-context monorepo. Use CONTEXT-MAP.md and record how each context should find its ADRs.

output_examples.input: Set up the repository for GitHub Issues and default labels.
output_examples.output: A new Agent skills section points to docs/agents. The issue tracker file says to use GitHub Issues, and triage labels keep the five defaults.

output_examples.input: Use local markdown issues and a single domain context.
output_examples.output: The setup records .scratch as the issue location. The domain documentation file points agents to the root CONTEXT.md and root ADR directory.

output_examples.input: Configure a custom tracker such as Linear.
output_examples.output: The setup asks for the workflow in prose and writes that process into docs/agents/issue-tracker.md for later skills to follow.

best_practices: Run the skill before using triage, PRD, issue, diagnosis, or architecture skills.
Use the label names that already exist in the tracker to avoid duplicates.
Review the proposed Agent skills block before allowing the skill to write files.
anti_patterns: Do not run setup without knowing where the team tracks work.
Do not accept default labels when the tracker already uses different names.
Do not duplicate an existing Agent skills section in AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a prompt-driven setup guide. It asks questions, shows a draft, and writes files after confirmation.

faq.question: Which issue trackers are built in?
faq.answer: It includes templates for GitHub, GitLab, and local markdown. Other trackers can be recorded as custom prose.

faq.question: Does it work with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What files can it create?
faq.answer: It can update AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md and create docs/agents/issue-tracker.md, triage-labels.md, and domain.md.

faq.question: Can it support monorepos?
faq.answer: Yes. It can record a multi-context layout that uses CONTEXT-MAP.md and context-specific ADR directories.

faq.question: Does it create tracker labels?
faq.answer: No. It records label names for other skills to use. Label creation remains a separate tracker administration task.
